---
title: Prisons in Figures. Just How Much Trouble Are We In?
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(data.table)
library(tidyverse)

library(ggthemes) # Load
library(tidyquant)


theme_lab <- function () { 
  theme_grey(base_size = 14, base_family = "sans") %+replace% 
    theme(
      # plot margin
      plot.margin = unit(rep(0.5, 4), "cm"),
      # plot background and border
      plot.background = element_blank(),
      panel.background = element_blank(),
      panel.border = element_blank(),
      # grid lines
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_line(size = 0.5, color = "#cbcbcb"), 
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      # axis ticks and lines
      axis.ticks = element_blank(),
      axis.line = element_blank(),
      # title, subtitle and caption
      plot.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold", colour = "#757575", hjust = 0),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 16, colour = "#757575", hjust = 0, margin = margin(9, 0, 9, 0)),
      plot.caption = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey50", hjust = 1, margin = margin(t = 15)),
      # axes titles
      axis.title = element_text(colour = "#757575", hjust = 1),
      axis.text.x = element_text(margin = margin(b = 7)),
      axis.text.y = element_text( margin = margin(l = 7)),
      # legend
      legend.position = "top",
      legend.background = element_blank(),
      legend.key = element_blank(),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 12, colour = "#757575"),
      legend.text.align = 0,
      legend.text = element_text(size = 14, colour = "#757575"),
      # facetting
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
      strip.text = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", colour = "#757575", hjust = 0)
    )
}

library(ggplot2) ; library(tidyverse) ; library(scales) ; library(svglite)

source("https://trafforddatalab.github.io/assets/theme/ggplot2/theme_lab.R")

theme_set(theme_lab())

```

![Derek Harper / *Wandsworth Prison* / [CC BY-SA 2.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0/)](prison.jpg)

```{r Prison Population}

data <- read_csv("prison_population.csv")
data$Month <- as.Date(data$Month, origin = "1899-12-30")
prison_population <- data[data$Sex == 'Both',] %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Month, y = Population)) +
  geom_line(size = 0.75, color = '#c8a2c8') + 
  labs(x = "Month", y = "Total Prisoners", title = "Prison Population at Month End \n (January 2009 to January 2024)")
prison_population
```

```{r Capacity Scatter}
data_2024 <- read_csv("capacity.csv")

data_2024$num_above_max <- data_2024$`Population *` - data_2024$`In Use CNA`

data_2024 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = `In Use CNA`, y = `Population *`)) +
  geom_point(aes(colour = cut(num_above_max, c(-Inf, 0, Inf)))) +
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linetype="dashed", color = 'darkblue') +
  labs(x = "Prison Capacity (In-Use CNA)", y = "Number of Prisoners", title = "Number of Prisoners Against Capacity \n in England & Wales (May 2024)", col = "Above or Below Capacity") +
theme(legend.position="none") +
scale_color_manual(values = c("#50C878", "#FF5733"))

```

```{r Capacity Histogram, fig.show="hold", out.width="50%"}

data <- read.csv("capacity_over_time.csv")
data$num_above_max <- data$Population.. - data$`In.Use.CNA`

data_2019 <- filter(data, Year == "2019")

plot <- data_2019 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=num_above_max)) + 
  geom_histogram(color="darkgrey", fill="#A7C7E7", binwidth = 50 ) +
  labs(x = "Number of Prisoners Above Capacity (Above In-Use CNA)", y = "Count", title = "Prison Overcapacity Distribution \n in England & Wales (December 2019)")

plot + geom_vline(aes(xintercept=mean(data_2019$num_above_max)),
             color="blue", linetype="dashed", size=1)

plot <- data_2024 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=num_above_max)) + 
  geom_histogram(color="darkgrey", fill="coral", binwidth = 50 ) +
  labs(x = "Number of Prisoners Above Capacity (Above In-Use CNA)", y = "Count", title = "Prison Overcapacity Distribution \n in England & Wales (May 2024)")

plot + geom_vline(aes(xintercept=mean(data_2024$num_above_max)),
             color="blue", linetype="dashed", size=1)





```

```{r Capacity Density, fig.show="hold", out.width="50%"}

ggplot(data_2019, aes(x=num_above_max)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=after_stat(density)), colour="darkgrey", fill="#A7C7E7", binwidth = 50)+
  geom_density(alpha=.2, fill="#FF6666") +
  labs(x = "Number of Prisoners Above Capacity (Above In-Use CNA)", y = "Density", title = "Prison Overcapacity Distribution \n in England & Wales (December 2019)")

ggplot(data_2024, aes(x=num_above_max)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(y=after_stat(density)), colour="darkgrey", fill="coral", binwidth = 50)+
  geom_density(alpha=.2, fill="#FF6666") +
  labs(x = "Number of Prisoners Above Capacity (Above In-Use CNA)", y = "Density", title = "Prison Overcapacity Distribution \n in England & Wales (May 2024)")




```

```{r capacity over time}
data <- read.csv("capacity_over_time.csv")
data$num_above_max <- data$Population.. - data$`In.Use.CNA`

data$Year <- as.factor(data$Year)
data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Year, y = num_above_max, color = Year)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Number of Prisoners Above Capacity \n (Above In-Use CNA)", title = "Prison Overcapacity \n in England & Wales (2019 - 2024)") + theme(legend.position="none")
```


```{r 2019 dists}


data_2019 <- filter(data, Year == "2019")

plot <- data_2019 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=num_above_max)) + 
  geom_histogram(color="darkgrey", fill="#A7C7E7", binwidth = 50 ) +
  labs(x = "Number of Prisoners Above Capacity (Above In-Use CNA)", y = "Count", title = "Prison Overcapacity Distribution \n in England & Wales (December 2019)")

plot <- plot + geom_vline(aes(xintercept=mean(num_above_max)),
             color="blue", linetype="dashed", size=1)

plot




```


```{r capacity for most overpopulated prisons over time}
prison_list <- c("Wandsworth", "Oakwood", "Pentonville", "Leeds", "Forest Bank", "Doncaster", "Durham", "Altcourse", "Hull", "Brixton")
short_prison_list <- c("Wandsworth", "Oakwood", "Pentonville", "Leeds", "Forest Bank")

data_sub <- subset(data, Prison.Name %in% short_prison_list)

data_sub |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Year, y = num_above_max, fill = Prison.Name)) +
  geom_bar(stat="identity", position=position_dodge()) + labs(x = "Year", y = "Number of Prisoners Above Capacity \n (Above In-Use CNA)", title = "Prison Overcapacity for Five Prisons \n in England & Wales (2019 - 2024)", color = "Prison") +
  scale_fill_discrete(name = "Prison") + theme(legend.key.size = unit(0.2, 'cm'))






```

```{r Types of Offence}

data <- read.csv("offence_types.csv")
data <- data[order(data$Number, decreasing = FALSE),]
data %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(`Offence.category`, -Number), y = Number, fill = `Offence.category`)) +
  geom_bar(stat = 'identity') + theme(legend.position="none") + coord_flip() +
  labs(y = "Prisoners Convicted of Offence", title = "Number of Prisoners by Type of Offence (March 2024)", color = "Prison")

```
